coverage_note: The bundled MT-RNR1 population database is literature and guideline oriented. Mitochondrial variants are not represented like diploid nuclear variants in many exome/genome population panels, and interpretation should consider heteroplasmy, haplogroup background, and maternal inheritance.
gene_population_patterns_intro: Broader population patterns curated from MT-RNR1 pharmacogenetic, hearing-loss, and maternal-lineage literature.

sources.label: NCBI Gene 4549: MT-RNR1 gene summary and literature links
NCBI Bookshelf: Gentamicin therapy and MT-RNR1 genotype

gene_population_patterns.summary: m.1555A>G is the most widely reported MT-RNR1 aminoglycoside ototoxicity allele, with prevalence and penetrance shaped by population background, aminoglycoside exposure, heteroplasmy, and modifying variants.

m.1494C>T is rarer than m.1555A>G but has strong guideline support as an increased-risk allele; many reported families and cohorts are East Asian, especially Chinese, though it is not exclusive to that ancestry.
m.1095T>C is curated as a CPIC increased-risk allele with more moderate evidence, so population interpretation should preserve uncertainty around penetrance and study size.
m.827A>G is included as a normal-risk contrast allele in CPIC aminoglycoside recommendations; this helps separate hearing-loss literature signals from expert pharmacogenetic risk categories.
